prompt_text: Best AI tooling alternatives for AI product teams building and iterating on LLM-powered product features teams on a budget
response:
# Budget-Friendly AI Tooling for LLM Product Teams

## Core Development & Experimentation

**LLM APIs & Models**
- **OpenAI API** - Most mature, but pricey. Good for proven features
- **Claude (Anthropic)** - Competitive pricing, excellent for reasoning
- **Open-source models** (Llama 2, Mistral, etc.) - Free but require hosting
  - Run locally with **Ollama** (free, easy setup)
  - Self-host on **Together AI** or **Replicate** (pay-per-use, cheaper than OpenAI)
- **Groq** - Very fast inference, free tier available

**Prompt Engineering & Testing**
- **Promptfoo** - Open-source, local testing framework (free)
- **LangSmith Lite** - Free tier for chains/agents
- **Braintrust** - Collaborative eval, generous free tier
- **GitHub Copilot** - If your team uses GitHub anyway

## Development Frameworks (Free/Open-Source)

- **LangChain** - Most mature, large community (free)
- **LlamaIndex** - RAG specialist, free tier
- **Pydantic AI** - Lightweight, type-safe (free)
- **Marvin** - Simpler alternative to LangChain (free)

## Data & Context Management

- **Supabase** - Postgres + vector embeddings, free tier generous
- **Pinecone** - Vector DB, $0.04/M tokens free tier
- **Qdrant** - Self-hosted vector DB (free)
- **Milvus** - Open-source vector DB (free)

## Monitoring & Observability

- **Langfuse** - Open-source LLM observability, free for small teams
- **Arize** - Free tier for model monitoring
- **Custom logging** - Simple structured logs to Supabase/BigQuery

## Evaluation & Quality

- **OpenAI Evals** - Framework for testing LLM outputs (free)
- **Ragas** - RAG evaluation library (free, open-source)
- **UpTrain** - Data quality checks (free tier)
- **Custom eval pipelines** - Claude for grading (cheaper than GPT-4)

## Deployment Options

- **Vercel** - Free tier for APIs/backends
- **Railway** - Cheap containers ($5/month minimum)
- **Modal** - Serverless Python, free tier for inference
- **Fly.io** - Generous free tier, pay-as-you-go
- **AWS Lambda** - Use Claude API within Lambda

## Analytics & User Feedback

- **Posthog** - Open-source product analytics, free tier
- **Mixpanel** - Generous free tier
- **Simple spreadsheets** - For small teams, seriously

## Money-Saving Tips

1. **Use open-source models for lower-stakes features** (customer support, content filtering)
2. **Batch API calls** - Use Claude Batch API for 50% savings on non-urgent tasks
3. **Cache prompts** - Both OpenAI & Anthropic offer caching discounts
4. **Start with free tiers**, only pay when you hit limits
5. **Use async/background jobs** - Don't run expensive LLM calls synchronously
6. **Implement smart fallbacks** - Cheaper model → expensive model only if needed
7. **Rate limit & quota management** - Prevent runaway costs

## Recommended Starter Stack (~$50-200/month)

- **Claude API** (usage-based) or self-hosted Mistral
- **LangChain** (free)
- **Supabase** (free tier + $25 if needed)
- **Promptfoo** (free)
- **Railway or Fly** ($5-20)
- **Langfuse** (free tier)

This gets you to production without breaking the bank. Scale up specific pieces as you validate product-market fit.

What type of feature are you building? I can recommend a more specific stack.
